channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Everything leaving Hulu in December 2024
item.description: Here's everything leaving Hulu in December 2024, and it's all movies, to make way for iconic Christmas titles for the festive season.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Dell expands AI capabilities with new PowerEdge servers using high-performance AMD processors
item.description: Dell's new PowerEdge servers look to accelerate deployment timelines and optimize performance for AI applications globally.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Tech Ecosystem Powering Latin America’s Growth
item.description: Even for those of us optimistic about Latin America’s (LATAM) business prospects, the path ahead isn’t without challenges. Political tensions persist. Several major economies in the region continue to seek balance and stability.
item.dc:creator: Nacho De Marco, Fast Company

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who had bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: My spur-of-the-moment Chromebook surprise
item.description: If you’ve read this column for long, you probably know that when it comes to tech purchases — and tech decisions in general, really — I’m typically not one to be hasty. It’s practically in my blood at this point.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Python 3.14 is a rational constant
item.description: This (half-) month in Python and elsewhere: Get your feet wet with Pi-thon, er, Python 3.14, in its alpha 2 release! See how standalone Python app executables work with PyInstaller. Use Python’s pattern matching to unlock type-based decision-making superpowers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Joshua trees are in peril. California has a plan to save them
item.description: The California Department of Fish and Wildlife has released a new plan to protect the state’s iconic Joshua trees, which are imperiled by wildfires, human development and climate change.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Home for the holidays? Show relatives you care with some tech support
item.description: As the holiday season kicks off, the journey home could also mean that those who are more tech-savvy will be called on to provide a backlog of tech support to parents, grandparents and other family.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Chen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The outlook is uncertain for AI regulations as the US government pivots to full Republican control
item.description: The federal government is about to transition from one that prioritized safeguards for artificial intelligence to one more focused on eliminating red tape.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: La inteligencia artificial trasciende al espacio
item.description: Investigadores del grupo AIDA procedentes de la Universidad Politécnica de Madrid (UPM) han plantado cara a problemas tradicionales del sector aeroespacial gracias a la innovación tecnológica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Matt Gaetz is dishing out career advice on Cameo
item.description: After announcing he would not return to Congress, Matt Gaetz is trying out a new career path: making personalized videos on Cameo.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fox host: Podcasters like Rogan have 'earned' seats in White House briefing room
item.description: Fox News host Will Cain said popular podcasters like Joe Rogan have “earned” seats in the White House briefing room. When discussing the potential for a shake-up in the James S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Talk in 2 languages with Apple Watch's real-time translation
item.description: Apple Watch's Translate app gives you your own personal interpreter, allowing you to have live two-way conversations in up to 20 different languages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Equinix to cut 3% of staff amidst the greatest demand for data center infrastructure ever
item.description: Even as demand for data infrastructure surges to an all-time high, Equinix is planning to lay off 3% of its workforce, suggesting a growing skills mismatch in the industry.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Australia’s lower House passes social media ban for under-16s
item.description: Communications Minister Michelle Rowland said the Senate would debate the bill later Wednesday. The major parties' support all but guarantees the legislation will pass in the Senate, where no party holds a majority of seats.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract
item.description: Potential bidders have a little under a month to offer feedback on the draft RFP for what DoD is calling the Advancing AI Multiple Award Contract. The post Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: 10 ways AI can make farmers’ jobs easier
item.description: For those who work the earth, AI can be an invaluable tool for making that work more efficient, sustainable and productive. AI helps farmers use the same tools they have been using for centuries, but with improved efficiency and focus.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco, NTT Data partner to simplify private 5G connectivity
item.description: Cisco and NTT Data are extending their partnership to offer customers more streamlined options for deploying private 5G services for enterprise connectivity. The partnership ties Cisco’s networking technologies to NTT Data’s Transatel network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biden administration finalizes nearly $8B in CHIPS funding for Intel
item.description: Intel will receive about $7.86 billion in funding from the CHIPS and Science Act to build new semiconductor manufacturing facilities across four states under its finalized agreement with the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cheap Black Friday deals cost to your privacy
item.description: As Black Friday approaches, you should know that retailers often sell consumers' personal information and purchase histories to third parties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
